jokes.text: D'oh! (screams)
jokes.setup: Opening scene with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration but completely expected

jokes.text: My mom got too fat to work at Hooters! They won't even let her park cars.
jokes.setup: Nelson explaining his family's financial troubles
jokes.punchline: Escalating revelation that his mom lost even the parking job at Hooters
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good escalation structure - specific and memorable

jokes.text: (tires screeching)
jokes.setup: Teacher asks if Nelson has money for field trip
jokes.punchline: Family literally drives away to avoid paying
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline - economical and unexpected escalation

jokes.text: Hey, don't tell us how to feel!
jokes.setup: Teacher saying students are excited about museum visit
jokes.punchline: Bart's rebellious rejection of being told his emotions
jokes.explanation: Good Bart characterization but fairly standard rebellious response

jokes.text: What do you care, Mrs. Krabappel... or should I say, Mrs. Crab apple?
jokes.setup: Milhouse acting uncharacteristically bold
jokes.punchline: Weak pun on teacher's name delivered with false confidence
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but works for Milhouse's awkward attempt at rebellion

jokes.text: Crab apple? I never thought of that! It totally works! How could I have been so blind?
jokes.setup: Milhouse's weak pun on her name
jokes.punchline: Teacher's over-enthusiastic reaction to the obvious pun
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - adult character being impressed by weak joke

jokes.text: Rollin', rollin', rollin' toxic barrel rollin' / they're so hot and glowin' / we'll die!
jokes.setup: Nuclear plant workers handling toxic waste
jokes.punchline: Singing 'Rawhide' theme with toxic waste lyrics ending in 'we'll die!'
jokes.explanation: Clever parody with dark workplace humor - well-constructed

jokes.text: Let them while away the afternoon spilling their beer on gullets and trousers whilst drooling over French postcards
jokes.setup: Burns continuing his plan to send workers to Moe's
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Victorian-era description of getting drunk and looking at pornography
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns voice - archaic language contrasted with crude activities

jokes.text: At this 'museum,' you won't see a Michelangelo, but you might see Michael Landon and Beverly D'Angelo
jokes.setup: Museum guide describing what visitors will see
jokes.punchline: Classical art reference subverted with TV/movie celebrities with similar names
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay setup/punchline structure with cultural references

jokes.text: Man, you've been huffing from the bart bag
jokes.setup: Bart suggests sneaking away from group
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's play on 'barf bag' using Bart's name to suggest he's being crazy
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Milhouse's character and the situation well

jokes.text: To old man Burns, who's paying us to drink because we're embarrassing! We suck! We suck.
jokes.setup: Homer making a toast at the bar
jokes.punchline: Completely honest assessment of why Burns is paying them to drink
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Homer's oblivious honesty about their situation

jokes.text: Ohh... a lot of that went in my lungs
jokes.setup: Homer drinking/toasting
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact observation about drinking technique gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with Homer's typical oblivious delivery

jokes.text: I'm honored to drink to Apu and, uh... Apulina
jokes.setup: Homer toasting Apu and his wife on their anniversary
jokes.punchline: Homer obviously doesn't know Manjula's name and makes up 'Apulina'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - trying to be nice but revealing ignorance

jokes.text: Including a bouquet of flowers, diamond earrings, and we're going to see Paris... Hilton. In Paris... Texas. On our way to Paris, France.
jokes.setup: Apu listing anniversary gifts for his wife
jokes.punchline: Each 'Paris' revelation gets more elaborate and impressive
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation with good misdirection and wordplay

jokes.text: Well... these charity address labels that came in the mail
jokes.setup: Asked what he planned to get Marge for their anniversary
jokes.punchline: Pathetically inadequate free mail labels as anniversary gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cheapness contrasted with Apu's elaborate gifts

jokes.text: No, Walter's been cool
jokes.setup: Bart asks if Milhouse's imaginary friend tried to kill him again
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to apparently ongoing murderous imaginary friend
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - treats homicidal imaginary friend as normal social update

jokes.text: Well, if it's not easy, don't do it. That's how I got where I am.
jokes.setup: Milhouse saying he needs to say something difficult
jokes.punchline: Bart's philosophy of avoiding difficulty with honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Bart's lazy philosophy stated with perfect honesty

jokes.text: You mean up until now you did care? Then why did you wear that tutu to school last week?
jokes.setup: Milhouse claiming he doesn't care what people think anymore
jokes.punchline: Bart questioning this with reference to recent embarrassing wardrobe choice
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction revealing Milhouse's past embarrassments

jokes.text: What about all the times I didn't wear a tutu? Nobody ever brings those up.
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out Milhouse's tutu-wearing
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's backwards logic about getting credit for normal behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic that perfectly captures Milhouse's desperate reasoning

jokes.text: It's the Paul Lynde Helen Reddy Hudson Brothers Easter Special! With guest stars Willie Tyler and Lester and Nadia Comaneci!
jokes.setup: Milhouse watching old TV
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific 1970s variety show with bizarre guest combination
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire of 1970s TV variety shows with random celebrity combinations

jokes.text: I don't think you understand the mechanics of heterosexual sex
jokes.setup: Paul Lynde's sexual innuendo to gymnast
jokes.punchline: Blunt, clinical response from Olympic athlete
jokes.explanation: Unexpected deadpan response subverts typical variety show banter

jokes.text: Check it out-- I'm riding some guy named Ironside!
jokes.setup: Bart playing with TV museum exhibits
jokes.punchline: Bart literally riding the wheelchair of detective show character
jokes.explanation: Visual gag playing on cultural reference to wheelchair-bound detective show

jokes.text: Ow, my banana! Danger, danger! Door ajar. The indignity!
jokes.setup: Robot being damaged in museum
jokes.punchline: Mix of Lost in Space robot catchphrase with car warning and dignity complaint
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination of robot responses mixing different cultural references

jokes.text: I don't wanna go home! I'm not done talking to me!
jokes.setup: Drunk Homer being kicked out of bar
jokes.punchline: Homer's drunken logic about having a conversation with himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk Homer logic - absurd but internally consistent reasoning

jokes.text: You think that rat is a remote
jokes.setup: Bum talking to drunk Homer on street
jokes.punchline: Homer so drunk he's trying to use a rat as TV remote
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Homer's extreme drunken state

jokes.text: I don't need your sharity!
jokes.setup: Someone offering Homer money
jokes.punchline: Drunk Homer's slurred 'charity' becomes 'sharity'
jokes.explanation: Simple drunk speech joke with Homer's dignity intact despite circumstances

jokes.text: I'll dance for my money! (humming hoochie-coochie)
jokes.setup: Homeless Homer rejecting charity
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by dancing for money instead
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing Homer's weak resolve and desperate situation

jokes.text: You think you can dance
jokes.setup: Homer dancing badly for money
jokes.punchline: Bum's brutal assessment of Homer's dancing ability
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective put-down that adds to Homer's humiliation

jokes.text: You can't move that far. You're my best friend. What's your mom making? I'll match it!
jokes.setup: Milhouse announcing he's moving to Capital City
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate offer to match his mom's salary to keep him
jokes.explanation: Good showing of Bart's desperation and childish understanding of economics

jokes.text: My mom's already transferred her 401k
jokes.setup: Bart trying to convince Milhouse not to move
jokes.punchline: Child referencing adult financial planning as proof of finality
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of child using sophisticated financial terminology

jokes.text: Hey, I thought you said my money was no good. I said you're no good.
jokes.setup: Kirk misunderstanding Luann's previous insult
jokes.punchline: Luann clarifying she was insulting him, not his money
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay setup and payoff showing Kirk's obtuseness

jokes.text: I'll fight you with every lunch half-hour I get
jokes.setup: Kirk threatening legal battle over Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Pathetic threat limited by his work lunch break schedule
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly pathetic Kirk line - shows his powerlessness through specific work constraints

jokes.text: Why are your clothes so dirty? And why do you smell like liquor? Have you been clubbing?
jokes.setup: Marge seeing disheveled Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent assumption that 'clubbing' explains Homer's state
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's naive interpretation of Homer's homeless appearance

jokes.text: That Chevron station has the most romantic bouquets!
jokes.setup: Marge receiving flowers from Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's genuine appreciation for gas station flowers
jokes.explanation: Shows Marge's sweet nature and low expectations, contrasted with romantic ideal

jokes.text: We're having a simultaneous pass out!
jokes.setup: Both Homer and Marge feeling faint at same time
jokes.punchline: Marge excitedly narrating their shared collapse
jokes.explanation: Absurd romantic moment with Marge finding pattern in chaos

jokes.text: My nose makes its own bubble gum!
jokes.setup: Ralph arriving as Bart's potential new friend
jokes.punchline: Ralph's typical non-sequitur about nasal secretions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph line - completely absurd but delivered with childlike wonder

jokes.text: I found you, Bart! Ralph, we're playing checkers. I don't like you, boy-mommy.
jokes.setup: Ralph interrupting Bart's checkers game
jokes.punchline: Ralph calling Bart 'boy-mommy' after being corrected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ralph insult - 'boy-mommy' is both absurd and somehow cutting

jokes.text: You smell worse than you did last time
jokes.setup: Bum seeing Homer again on the street
jokes.punchline: Casual observation that Homer's hygiene is deteriorating
jokes.explanation: Simple callback showing Homer's declining state through bum's perspective

jokes.text: Well, there are six schools of begging: Bad musician, messed-up vet, cripple, fake cripple, religious zealot, and crazy guy
jokes.setup: Homer asking for begging advice
jokes.punchline: Comprehensive taxonomy of panhandling strategies
jokes.explanation: Excellent systematic approach to begging with dark humor and social observation

jokes.text: Coke and Pepsi are the same thing! Wake up, people!
jokes.setup: Homer trying the 'crazy guy' begging approach
jokes.punchline: Homer's version of conspiracy theory about cola brands
jokes.explanation: Good Homer take on conspiracy theorist - mundane topic delivered with passion

jokes.text: Yeah. I gave my look some new flava. Suck it in!
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying to be cool in Capital City
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at urban slang and posturing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Milhouse's failed attempt at coolness

jokes.text: Now, please let me give you a wedgie in front of these guys. No way! Please? I'll be gentle.
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying to look cool in front of city kids
jokes.punchline: Pleading to be allowed to bully his best friend, promising gentleness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Milhouse's desperation and politeness even in fake bullying

jokes.text: Springfield baby, in a diaper, poked his eye with a windshield wiper! Springfield baby! Springfield baby!
jokes.setup: City kids mocking Bart after wedgie
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insulting chant about Springfield being infantile
jokes.explanation: Decent playground insult with rhythm and imagery, shows city vs. town dynamic

jokes.text: You'll be like an owl saying Milhouse who? Milhouse who? Milhouse whooo?
jokes.setup: Marge trying to cheer up Bart about missing Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Marge's extended owl sound pun to help Bart forget
jokes.explanation: Sweet Marge attempt at wordplay, though the joke is somewhat forced

jokes.text: Hey, moldilocks, mom says I'm supposed to help you. Fine. You can confirm the accuracy of the hose.
jokes.setup: Bart reluctantly helping Lisa wash car
jokes.punchline: Lisa's technical way of saying 'hold the hose' to give Bart minimal role
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic with Lisa's intellectual way of delegating menial task

jokes.text: You wet your pants. Shut up! It's a serious problem.
jokes.setup: Lisa gets sprayed with hose water
jokes.punchline: Lisa's defensive response treating it as medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's tendency to intellectualize embarrassment

jokes.text: I'm gonna hit you so hard, I'll kill your whole family! Bart, you're in my family. Shut up!
jokes.setup: Lisa threatening revenge on Bart
jokes.punchline: Realizes threat includes herself, dismisses logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Lisa's logic breaking down in anger, then dismissing it

jokes.text: Are these diamondique? Nope. Diamondelle? Nope. Cubic diamondium? Nope. Dioxy-ribo-diamondoid? Close, but no cigar.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to identify Homer's gift earrings
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate fake diamond names, with Homer playing along
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with increasingly ridiculous fake gemstone names

jokes.text: Hostess Twinkies? I heard, if you age them for ten years, they turn to liquor
jokes.setup: Homer looking in safe for what to put with diamonds
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre Twinkie aging theory
jokes.explanation: Good Homer pseudo-science combining his love of junk food and alcohol

jokes.text: Everyone knows you're the future of family. That's not true, Bart. Mom and dad value us equally, and... aw, you're right.
jokes.setup: Bart telling Lisa she should go first because she's more important
jokes.punchline: Lisa's principled objection followed by immediate capitulation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's idealism versus practical acceptance

jokes.text: Just forget everything you know about gravity. But I know so much about it.
jokes.setup: Bart giving Lisa jumping advice
jokes.punchline: Lisa's concern about abandoning her extensive physics knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa line - her intellectual pride even in dangerous situations

jokes.text: We're like Howard Carter discovering the temple of Tutankhamun. Or like when I discovered the school's xerox code. One, four, seven. Just saying it makes my butt feel warm again.
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing their discovery to famous archaeology
jokes.punchline: Bart's much more mundane but personally significant discovery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between Lisa's intellectual reference and Bart's practical mischief memory

jokes.text: Oh, look! He's picking his nose
jokes.setup: Looking at ancient pictographs
jokes.punchline: Bart's juvenile interpretation of sacred art
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart juvenile humor, predictable but in character

jokes.text: That's what you call commitment to a bit
jokes.setup: Lisa has spiders in her hair from handshake prank
jokes.punchline: Bart's meta-commentary on his own prank dedication
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Bart acknowledging the lengths he goes to for pranks

jokes.text: Thank god we've come to our senses and worship a carpenter who lived 2,000 years ago
jokes.setup: Reading about ancient animal worship
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dry observation about relative absurdity of modern religion
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation with Lisa's typical intellectual wit and skepticism

jokes.text: Like Englishmen and Scots. Or Welshmen and Scots. Or Japanese and Scots. Or Scots and other Scots. Damn Scots! They ruined Scotland!
jokes.setup: Willie explaining natural enemies
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending with Scots being their own worst enemy
jokes.explanation: One of the most quoted Simpsons jokes - perfect escalation structure with Willie's character voice

jokes.text: There's your husband, A.K.A. Mooch-a-lini, drooly McGee, corporal flashback, etcetera, etcetera
jokes.setup: Bum pointing out Homer to Marge
jokes.punchline: Creative homeless nicknames for Homer, especially 'Mooch-a-lini'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with 'Mooch-a-lini' combining Mussolini with mooching behavior

jokes.text: The bob seger boxed set, which really only needed to be one disk, but the box was nice...
jokes.setup: Homer listing anniversary gifts he bought Marge
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of unnecessary music compilation padding
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about music industry marketing tactics

jokes.text: Well, then maybe you need to buy me a brooch
jokes.setup: Homer not understanding Marge's earring punishment
jokes.punchline: Marge's threat of making Homer buy more jewelry as punishment
jokes.explanation: Good Marge logic - threatening to accept more gifts as punishment

jokes.text: The judge said I was the most pathetic person he'd ever seen in court. Pity custody-- boo-yeah!
jokes.setup: Kirk explaining how he got Milhouse back
jokes.punchline: Kirk's pride in winning custody through being pathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kirk moment - turning personal humiliation into victory with 'boo-yeah!'

jokes.text: You're somebody's father?! Yes, okay. Ooh, boy.
jokes.setup: Repo man about to take Kirk's pants
jokes.punchline: Repo man's horror at discovering Kirk has reproduced
jokes.explanation: Great reaction beat showing how pathetic Kirk appears to strangers

jokes.text: We were having a sleepover, and a robber came and wet my bed. Then he folded the bed back into the couch and disappeared into the night.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining why Capital City kids don't think he's cool
jokes.punchline: Absurd story where even the robber is more competent than Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist story - even criminals are more thoughtful than Milhouse is competent

jokes.text: When a woman says nothing's wrong, that means everything's wrong. And when a woman says everything's wrong, that means everything's wrong. And when a woman says something's not funny, you'd better not laugh your ass off.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining women's communication to Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'wisdom' about impossible female logic ending with specific threat
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor with good escalation structure, predictable but well-delivered

jokes.text: This is what sitcoms call 'a schmaltzy ending'-- a sentimental capper to leave the audience feeling good. Usually followed by a little coda to cut the treacle.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa having emotional moment
jokes.punchline: Show directly explaining its own structure and emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary breaking fourth wall to explain sitcom conventions

jokes.text: Granny, I'm gonna shoot me some Vietcong. Yeah, well I ain't cookin' 'em.
jokes.setup: Old TV show playing as the coda
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of war violence with domestic response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from old TV show serving as the 'treacle-cutting' coda mentioned by narrator
